title: santolina insularis
intro: Santolina insularis, also known as Canary Island grey cotton or Santolina, is a perennial shrubby plant native to the Canary Islands and Mediterranean regions. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is known for its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Santolina insularis can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
